description: Recto: Letter/petition, or letter in the style of a brief petition, from "your student Bū l-Majd" to a high-ranking physician. In Arabic script. Dating: Probably ca. 13th century, based on format, typical name, and handwriting. The writer asks the addressee in formal terms to come visit him, because he has developed a pain in his leg. The door of the building is open. Verso: A few words in calligraphic Judaeo-Arabic, mostly "tajriba" repeated several times. ASE.
tags: DIMME, illness letter 969-1517, Arabic script, tarjama, Illness, Illness: treatment, Illness: physician
description_he: מכתב בArabic, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בכיא פריז.
